# SRC017 - Banner Blindness Revisited

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Buyer Psychology |
| Authors / source | Nielsen Norman Group |
| Year | 2018 |
| Source type | UX research / eye-tracking synthesis |
| Quality | Medium-High |
| URL | [https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banner-blindness-old-and-new-findings/](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banner-blindness-old-and-new-findings/) |

## Bias / limitation

UX consultancy; strong observational value.

## Core takeaway

Users ignore ad-like elements, ad-like locations, and nearby ad clutter.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to design native, useful, context-matched creative and landing pages.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC017
- What it changes about our strategy:

- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
